title: Cereus millspaughii
intro: Cereus millspaughii, also known as the 'Cardón cactus', is a species of columnar cactus native to the Caribbean and Central America, characterized by its tall, slender stems and sharp spines. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Cereus millspaughii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cereus millspaughii is often used to make infusions, teas, and salves, and its extracts are also used in modern pharmaceutical applications.
taxonomy: Cereus millspaughii is classified within the family Cactaceae. This plant is further classified into the subfamily Cactoideae and tribe Cereae. The genus Cereus is a group of columnar cacti, and Cereus millspaughii is one of its species. Cereus millspaughii is native to Mexico and is often found in dry, rocky areas. Its classification helps in understanding its unique characteristics and adaptations.
